title: JavaScript Page Printing Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with printing and document handling.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in JavaScript for triggering the browser's print dialog?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
Using the .window.print() method to open the built-in print dialog for the current page.
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using JavaScript to trigger printing compared to a regular website?
options: It makes your HTML files smaller and more readable.
It allows users to print only the core content (like an article or receipt) with a single click.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is 'window.print()' inside a professional web application?
options: To make the site load much faster.
A JavaScript function that opens the browser's standard print preview window for the current URL.
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically generate CSS animations.

questions.question: Why is it important to almost ALWAYS avoid 'Universal Selectors' everywhere in your professional JavaScript printing logic?
options: They are difficult to maintain.
They can make the site load much slower and potentially confuse the printing layout.
They are not supported in modern browsers.
They take up no space in the browser.

questions.question: Which of these is a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet for JavaScript page printing?
options: Commenting every single line of code.
Using a reset or normalize stylesheet.
Using '@media print' CSS rules to perfectly format exactly what should (and shouldn't) be printed for the user.
Using variables for colors and spacing.
